mnemonics: CLUSTER: Collection of Linked Units Sorted To Enhance Retrieval
keyPoints: The cluster model groups similar documents together into clusters based on their content.
Retrieval is more efficient because a query is compared to cluster centroids (representatives) first, rather than every single document.
It is an alternative to the inverted index model, aiming to improve scalability for very large collections.
commonMistakes: Confusing the cluster model with clustering algorithms used for data analysis; here it is a specific IR retrieval method.
Assuming all documents in a cluster are always perfectly relevant to a query matched against the cluster centroid.
Forgetting that building and maintaining the clusters adds an initial overhead compared to a simple inverted index.
